<blockquote>
'''''Figure:''' Approximating the [[Glossary:Stability_increase_matrix|SInc[] matrix]] with the best-fit function used by default in SuperMemo to compute the increase in [[Glossary:Stability|stability]] (e.g. in cases of lack of data). The approximation procedure uses a [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hill_climbing hill-climbing algorithm] with parameters '''A''', '''B''', '''C''', '''D''' displayed in the picture. Least squares deviation is obtained to assess the progress. <span style="padding: 3px; background-color: lime;">Green circles</span> represent the [[Glossary:Stability_increase_matrix|Sinc[] matrix]] at a chosen [[Glossary:Difficulty|difficulty level]]. Their size corresponds with repetition cases investigated. The <span style="padding: 3px; color: #FFF; background-color: blue;">blue surface</span> is the best fit of the studied function to the SInc[] data.''
</blockquote>
